mission.title: rook: Mon canary pod remains pending even when not needed anymore
mission.description: Mon canary pod remains pending even when not needed anymore. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your rook deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
**Is this a bug report or feature request?**
* Bug Report

**Deviation from expected behavior:**
The mon canary pod may remain in a pending state even if not needed.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Mon canary pod remains pending even when not needed anymore
steps.description:
~With this modification the mon failover process is not executed if there is
no available nodes where to schedule the mon pod.
This avoid to have mon canary pods in Pending state because no nodes
available.~

If for any cause the operator processing a mon failover is restarted, the
mon canary pods
```yaml
rook-ceph-mon-d-canary remained in pending state even after other 3  rook-ceph-mon pods came up and running.


$ oc -n openshift-storage get pods | grep rook-ceph-mon
rook-ceph-mon-a-84ddfd7588-vvxr4                1/1     Running     1          5h53m
rook-ceph-mon-b-57b794b755-wx6x8                1/1     Running     0          4h38m
rook-ceph-mon-c-5bb7549576-cx4xr                1/1     Running     1          5h52m
rook-ceph-mon-d-canary-59d9d74795-k9s56         0/1     Pending     0          146m
```

steps.title: Upgrade rook to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade rook:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade rook rook/rook --namespace rook
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook
helm list -n rook
```

steps.title: Confirm Mon canary pod remains pending even when not… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
~With this modification the mon failover process is not executed if there is
no available nodes where to schedule the mon pod.
codeSnippets:
rook-ceph-mon-d-canary remained in pending state even after other 3  rook-ceph-mon pods came up and running.


$ oc -n openshift-storage get pods | grep rook-ceph-mon
rook-ceph-mon-a-84ddfd7588-vvxr4                1/1     Running     1          5h53m
rook-ceph-mon-b-57b794b755-wx6x8                1/1     Running     0          4h38m
rook-ceph-mon-c-5bb7549576-cx4xr                1/1     Running     1          5h52m
rook-ceph-mon-d-canary-59d9d74795-k9s56         0/1     Pending     0          146m

# kubectl -n rook-ceph describe pod rook-ceph-operator-844c546bb5-rdwps  | grep Node:
Node:         ku-worker-00.karmalabs.com/192.168.122.132

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
